item.title: House of Satoshi - Bitcoin und Friends вҖ“ #59 CARF: AuslГӨndische Krypto-Plattformen melden deine Daten - mit Thomas Linder
item.description:
**Diese Episode wird von PostFinance prГӨsentiert***

Dein Krypto-Update: Der monatliche Krypto-Newsletter von PostFinance ordnet die wichtigsten Entwicklungen rund um Bitcoin, Ethereum und Co. kompakt ein. рҹ‘ү Jetzt abonnieren: https://www.postfinance.ch/de/privat/anlegen/moeglichkeiten/krypto/newsletter-abonnieren.html#/category?groupId=newsletter_krypto

********************************************************

Ab 2027 melden auslГӨndische Krypto-Plattformen deine Daten automatisch an die Schweizer SteuerbehГ¶rde . Rino spricht mit Steuerspezialisten Thomas Linder von MME darГјber, was CARF konkret heisst, wer betroffen ist und warum jetzt der Moment zum Handeln ist.

Das Wichtigste in dieser Folge:

  • CARF lГӨuft wie der AIA fГјrs Bankkonto, einfach fГјr Krypto. Nicht du meldest, sondern der Dienstleister.
  • Wer nie deklariert hat, sollte jetzt Гјber die straflose Selbstanzeige nachdenken. Danach drohen Bussen bis zum FГјnffachen.
  • Self-Custody und DeFi bleiben vorerst aussen vor, ^und die USA fГӨhrt ihr eigenes System.
  • Die grГ¶ssere Frage ist die PrivatsphГӨre, ganz ohne breite Debatte durchgewunken.

Gast: Thomas Linder, Tax Partner bei MME, leitet dort das DLT-Team und ist seit zehn Jahren im Schweizer Krypto-Steuerrecht zu Hause.


Abonniere unseren Newsletter fГјr Bitcoin-Insights, praktische Tipps und viel Wissen aus dem House of Satoshi: https://house-of-satoshi.ch/newsletter

]]>

item.title: The Bitcoin Layer вҖ“ I Woke Up Homeless With $75 When the Banks Took Everything | with Tony Yazbek
item.description: <![CDATA[In this episode, Nik welcomes Tony Yazbek, co-founder of The Bitcoin Way, for a conversation on why owning bitcoin yourself is the whole point. Tony argues that bitcoin is money rather than an investment, and that anything wrapped in legacy finance, from ETFs to exchange balances, is not the same as holding it directly. He recounts surviving Lebanon's 2019 banking collapse, when he woke up homeless with about $75 after the banks confiscated everyone's savings, and how it shaped his conviction. Tony and Nik also dig into what self-sovereignty does for people, how to think about hardware and air-gapped storage, the volatility myth, and jurisdictional sovereignty, with Tony drawing on 30 years in cybersecurity throughout.]]>

He recounts surviving Lebanon's 2019 banking collapse, when he woke up homeless with about $75 after the banks confiscated everyone's savings, and how it shaped his conviction. Tony and Nik also dig into what self-sovereignty does for people, how to think about hardware and air-gapped storage, the volatility myth, and jurisdictional sovereignty, with Tony drawing on 30 years in cybersecurity throughout.]]>

item.title: Coin Stories with Natalie Brunell вҖ“ Michael Saylor: Answering the Critics on mNAV, Bitcoin Per Share, and the Path to $1 Million
item.description:
Natalie Brunell sits down with Strategy's Michael Saylor at BTC Prague, just after he took heat for selling 32 Bitcoin вҖ” and for recent comments defending how he measures the company's growth and what its stock is really worth.

Saylor answers the critics and short sellers head-on, explaining why "never sell your Bitcoin" is advice for everyday holders, no longer a rule for a company built to buy Bitcoin and pay out Bitcoin-backed dividends. We discuss:

  • Why "never sell your Bitcoin" is advice for individual retail investors
  • How the company is built to survive a crash
  • Why he argues Bitcoin needs credit to keep winning
  • The four ideologies of Bitcoin
  • When he expects capital to rotate back from AI into Bitcoin

Follow Michael Saylor on X https://x.com/saylor

item.title: Store of Bitcoin - Bitcoin as Store of Value вҖ“ Ep. 49 - Daniel Batten | A new different wave of Bitcoin Adopters.
item.description:
In this episode of Store of Bitcoin, host Alessandro Ottaviani welcomes Daniel Batten, a prominent Bitcoin analyst and investor, to discuss the evolving narrative surrounding Bitcoin, particularly in relation to energy consumption and environmental impact. Daniel shares insights on how the mainstream media's perception of Bitcoin has shifted from skepticism to a more favorable view, largely due to rigorous research and advocacy from the Bitcoin community. He emphasizes the importance of effectively communicating Bitcoin's benefits to various stakeholders, including policymakers and investors, to foster broader adoption.

The conversation delves into the challenges of reaching new adopters, particularly those who may not be motivated by the same values that initially attracted early Bitcoin enthusiasts. Daniel highlights the need for a more nuanced approach to education and outreach, focusing on the social and environmental benefits of Bitcoin. He also discusses the potential for Bitcoin to serve as a tool for financial inclusion in developing countries, where it can provide a safeguard against inflation and empower individuals in oppressive regimes. The episode concludes with a look at future trends in Bitcoin adoption and the importance of aligning messaging with the values of diverse audiences.

This episode is sponsored by вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ BitVaultвҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ вҒ , the new open-source & self-custody wallet utilizing time-delays and multisignature to offset physical attacks and hacks.

item.title: The Bitcoin Infinity Show вҖ“ Bitcoin Core's Governance Problem | Secure Sovereign | Bitcoin Infinity Show #207
item.description:
Josh, AKA Secure Sovereign, joins the Bitcoin Infinity Show to talk about Bitcoin Core governance, the case for alternative node implementations, and Bitcoin Commons, a new Rust implementation he says specifies and locks down Bitcoin consensus while making it far easier to fork and run your own node. Josh recovered a life-changing stack of Bitcoin he had held since 2010, then turned to analyzing Bitcoin Core as an informal oligarchy, running a full-chain analysis of BIP-110 across more than 900,000 blocks and building selective synchronization so node runners can skip non-monetary data. The conversation covers self-custody, consensus specification, mining decentralization, the spam debate, and a commons-based governance model drawn from Ostrom and the Hanseatic League aimed at keeping Bitcoin sound money.

Connect with Secure Sovereign:

https://x.com/secsovereign

item.description:

В 

In this episode:

In Episode 220 of The Bitcoin Fortress Podcast, we examine the most important Bitcoin developments from June 7вҖ“14, 2026. We discuss the return of ETF inflows, the growing dominance of BlackRock and Fidelity in the Bitcoin ETF market, StrategyвҖҷs latest Bitcoin purchase and the evolving treasury-company landscape, mining profitability and the recent difficulty adjustment, stablecoin regulation under the GENIUS Act, the ongoing CLARITY Act debate, and the broader implications of AI, surveillance, and digital freedom. We also explore the major themes emerging from the Bitcoin podcast universe, including inflation, private-company Bitcoin treasuries, operational security, and post-quantum Bitcoin research. Finally, we feature A Bitcoin Way of Life вҖ“ Week 4, reflect on Bitcoin as a discipline rather than merely an investment, and close with a reminder that while the fiat world grows increasingly complex, Bitcoin continues to do exactly what it was designed to doвҖ”produce blocks and provide a foundation for financial sovereignty.

item.title: THE Bitcoin Podcast вҖ“ The Praxeology of Privacy: Freedom Tech vs Mass Surveillance | Max Hillebrand
item.description:
Privacy isnвҖҷt about having something to hide. ItвҖҷs about preserving the freedom to act, communicate, and coordinate without coercion.

In this episode of THE Bitcoin Podcast, Walker sits down with privacy advocate, cypherpunk, and Freedom Tech builder Max Hillebrand to discuss his new book, The Praxeology of Privacy.

Max explains how Austrian economics and cypherpunk philosophy intersect, why privacy is a natural consequence of self-ownership and property rights, and how surveillance distorts economic calculation in much the same way that money printing distorts markets.

The conversation explores:

  • Why privacy is fundamentally tied to freedom
  • The economic consequences of surveillance
  • CBDCs and the future of financial control
  • Why corporations and governments increasingly operate as surveillance partners
  • The role of Bitcoin in creating a parallel economy
  • Tor, VPNs, encryption, and network-level privacy
  • NostrвҖҷs growing ecosystem and why developer adoption matters more than user counts
  • Operational security (OpSec) in both the digital and physical world
  • SignalвҖҷs limitations and the future of decentralized private communications
  • White Noise, Marmot, and the next generation of unstoppable encrypted messaging
  • How Freedom Tech can make coercion more expensive and liberty more accessible

Max also shares his vision for a future where money, communication, identity, and publishing are all decentralized, permissionless, and resistant to censorship.

Whether youвҖҷre new to privacy or already deep down the cypherpunk rabbit hole, this conversation provides a powerful framework for understanding why privacy mattersвҖ”and what practical steps you can take to reclaim it.

FOLLOW MAX:

NOSTR: https://primal.net/maxhillebrand

Try White Noise Chat: https://www.whitenoise.chat/

https://towardsliberty.com/

GET HIS BOOK:

The Praxeology of Privacy on Amazon: https://tinyurl.com/2xn9bs8y

Read on Nostr: https://tinyurl.com/4b42kb7d

  • NostrвҖҷs growing ecosystem and why developer adoption matters more than user counts
  • Operational security (OpSec) in both the digital and physical world
  • SignalвҖҷs limitations and the future of decentralized private communications
  • White Noise, Marmot, and the next generation of unstoppable encrypted messaging
  • How Freedom Tech can make coercion more expensive and liberty more accessible

Max also shares his vision for a future where money, communication, identity, and publishing are all decentralized, permissionless, and resistant to censorship.

Whether youвҖҷre new to privacy or already deep down the cypherpunk rabbit hole, this conversation provides a powerful framework for understanding why privacy mattersвҖ”and what practical steps you can take to reclaim it.

item.title: Bitcoin Magazine Podcast вҖ“ Calle on Bitchat: Messaging When The Internet is Shut Down
item.description:
What happens to communication when the internet gets shut off? Frank Corva sits down with Calle вҖ” creator of Cashu and a contributor to BitChat вҖ” to unpack two of the most exciting open-source projects in Bitcoin. They dig into how Cashu ecash matured from experimental tech into a real economy, and why BitChat's Bluetooth mesh network took off in Jamaica, Iran, and Nepal. Calle also explains why ecash may become the native money of AI agents.



Grab your copy THE 2036 ISSUEрҹ‘Үhttps://store.bitcoinmagazine.com/pages/the-2036-issue

рҹ”¶ Frank Corva - Independent Content Producerрҹ”¶ Creator of Cashu and contributor to BitChat



Chapters:


00:56 вҖ” The state of Cashu ecash and building the foundation

02:07 вҖ” Who's actually adopting ecash and why

03:26 вҖ” Ecash as "pocket money" for AI agents

04:50 вҖ” BitChat goes global: Jamaica, Iran, Nepal

05:35 вҖ” Why "build it and they will come" isn't a law

07:45 вҖ” Staying connected when the internet is shut down

09:00 вҖ” How far BitChat's mesh network can really reach

11:32 вҖ” Nostr, geohash chats, and going online

12:46 вҖ” What's next: open-source AI agents



#Bitcoin #Cashu #BitChat #Ecash #Calle #MITBitcoinExpo #BitcoinPrivacy #MeshNetworking #Nostr #OpenSource #Bluetooth #AIagents #BitcoinMagazine #Satoshis #Privacy #DecentralizedTech #FreeSpeech #LightningNetwork #Meshtastic #BitcoinNews #SelfSovereign #BitcoinLayer2 #ZeusWallet #OfflineMessaging

item.title: BITCOIN & ALTCOINS вҖ“ Funke oder FehlzГјndung? Was Kryptos jetzt antreibt

Positive Signale von SpaceX, ein greifbares Friedensabkommen mit dem Iran und die erste Fed-Pressekonferenz unter Kevin Warsh вҖ“ die Stimmung an den MГӨrkten hellt sich spГјrbar auf. item.title: The Bitcoin Treasury Podcast вҖ“ The Hidden Cost of a Large Bitcoin Buy
item.description:
The most common mistake in a first Bitcoin allocation is not the price paid. It is assuming the execution will take care of itself.

Teams make the buy decision, line up the approval, and then route a seven-figure order through the same retail venue they would use to buy a few hundred dollars of Bitcoin. The order fills, but not at the price they saw. The gap between the quoted price and what they actually paid can be significant, and on a large order it is real money.

Buying Bitcoin is operationally straightforward. Buying it well, at size, is not.

Michael Geraci, derivatives trader at Secure Digital Markets, covers the exchange, execution, and OTC relationships institutional Bitcoin holders need to understand before the first dollar moves. Will Reeves, CEO of Fold, joins with the operatorвҖҷs view from running a public company that holds Bitcoin on its own balance sheet.

вҖңAt a million dollars and up is really where the OTC markets offer depth and liquidity.вҖқВ  вҖ” Michael Geraci, Secure Digital Markets

What this session covers

When a retail exchange stops being enough

  • В  В  Past a certain size, execution cost becomes real. Slippage becomes material around $250K in notional and is unambiguous past $1M, where retail order books cannot absorb the trade cleanly.
  • В  В  A $1M Bitcoin buy typically runs 5 to 60 bps in execution cost. On a single trade that looks small; across a recurring accumulation program it is the difference that decides your cost basis.
  • В  В  OTC desks exist to solve this, offering real depth, bilateral pricing, and a counterparty accountable on the other end of the phone.

How a large order actually gets worked

  • В  В  Size is worked, not clicked. A meaningful order is sliced across venues and over time rather than dropped into a single book that it would move against itself.
  • В  В  Execution is benchmarked. Working an order against TWAP or VWAP lets it track the market instead of running ahead of it, which is the difference between a fill a treasurer can defend to a board and one they cannot.
  • В  В  Discretion matters. Bilateral pricing keeps a large order off the public tape, so the trade does not signal its own size to the rest of the market.

The operational groundwork that makes execution clean

  • В  В  Tier-1 banking relationships matter. Fiat needs to move cleanly, and wires to crypto venues are routinely flagged or blocked at banks that are not set up for them. Established banking rails remove that friction.
  • В  В  Work with a firm that picks up the phone. Setting up to trade at size involves real onboarding, banking, and compliance questions, and the answers are specific to each entity. A client-centric desk walks you through them with a named person on the other end of the line, rather than leaving you to a support inbox or a contact form.
  • В  В  Settlement speed is an edge. Pre-funded trades that settle T+0 to T+1 reduce the window in which anything can go wrong, rather than leaving funds in transit for days.
  • В  В  Flexibility is the point of a desk. Bespoke, bilateral structuring gives a treasury options a public order book cannot, from how an order is filled to how a position is later hedged or financed.

Get these pieces right and the first allocation stops being a leap of faith. The price you pay is defensible, the execution is clean, and you have a counterparty who answers when you call. From there, the same desk can help the position do more than sit, structuring yield, downside protection, and financing against Bitcoin you already hold, which is exactly the role a desk like SDM is built to play.

About Secure Digital Markets

SDM is a crypto-native institutional brokerage offering spot execution, derivatives, and structured lending. It specializes in bilateral transactions that give treasuries the depth, flexibility, and privacy a retail exchange cannot match, while clients retain custody of their own assets. You can learn more at SDM.co.

]]>

item.title: bitcoin ist - Wissen statt FOMO вҖ“ 18) ... Deflation fГјr alle
item.description:
Warum schlagen Г–konomen und Zentralbanken sofort Alarm, wenn sie das Wort вҖһDeflationвҖң hГ¶ren? Sie warnen vor Wirtschaftskrisen, hoher Arbeitslosigkeit und dem Zusammenbruch der MГӨrkte. Und in unserem aktuellen Finanzsystem haben sie damit sogar recht вҖ“ allerdings tun sie damit dem Begriff der Deflation unrecht.

In dieser Folge von вҖһbitcoin ist...вҖң schauen wir uns den Begriff der Deflation genauer an. Denn es gibt zwei Arten von Deflation: Eine, die ein krankes System kollabieren lГӨsst, und eine, die der absolute Normalzustand der menschlichen Zivilisation sein sollte.

  • Der "Fehler" im System: Warum unser schuldenbasiertes Geldsystem Angst vor sinkenden Preisen hat und warum Kredite niemals вҖһteurerвҖң werden dГјrfen.

  • Die gute Deflation: Wie der technologische Fortschritt (vom Rad bis zum Internet) unser Leben natГјrlicherweise immer effizienter und gГјnstiger machen mГјsste.

  • Das 2%-Mantra: Warum Zentralbanken die Geldmenge kГјnstlich ausweiten, um den ProduktivitГӨtsgewinn deiner Lebenszeit unbemerkt abzuschГ¶pfen.

  • Bitcoin als LГ¶sung: Wie eine absolut begrenzte Geldmenge von 21 Millionen Einheiten dafГјr sorgt, dass der menschliche Fortschritt endlich wieder direkt in deinem Geldbeutel landet.

вҖһDie Zentralbanken bekГӨmpfen die schlechte Deflation, indem sie permanent Geld drucken. Doch damit zerstГ¶ren sie gleichzeitig die gute Deflation вҖ“ sie fressen den technologischen Fortschritt einfach auf und verwГӨssern unsere Kaufkraft schleichend.вҖң

Alles von "bitcoin ist..." Hier geht es zu allen meinen KanГӨlen: https://linktr.ee/bitcoinist

Einsteiger-Spezial: mГ¶chtest du wissen, WIE man bitcoin sicher kauft und verwahrt? kostenlose und sichere Kaufanleitung direkt in dein Postfach

Hinterlasse mir gerne eine 5-Sterne-Bewertung oder einen Kommentar. Danke, Dein Christian.


]]>

War, inflation, and growing government surveillance have made a вҖңPlan BвҖқ residency more essential than ever. In this special edition of Bitcoin Banter, Michael and Tony sit down with entrepreneur Adam Hudson to count down the top 5 reasons you should get a second residency, citizenship, or passport abroad. This episode breaks down how to build real freedom and financial sovereignty.

Drawing on AdamвҖҷs own journey from Australia to Panama, the conversation covers offshore residency, second passports, territorial tax systems, self-custody Bitcoin, privacy and cybersecurity, and how everyday people (not just the rich) can create an exit plan in an increasingly restrictive world. Whether youвҖҷre an Australian, a Bitcoiner, or anyone seeking more freedom and optionality, this is your roadmap to getting started.

item.title: State of Bitcoin with Brandon Keys вҖ“ The European Bitcoin Ban Hiding In Plain Sight with Matyas Kuchar - State of Bitcoin Ep. 238
item.description:
Until last year, more than 1,000 restaurants in Czech Republic accepted Bitcoin. Then it all stopped.

The MiCA regulation didn't ban Bitcoin in Europe. It did something far more effective вҖ” it made the rails impossible to operate. And as the Czech merchant economy quietly disappeared, two bigger forces started moving in the background: the petrodollar dying, and CBDCs coming online in Europe THIS YEAR.

In this episode, I sit down with Matyas Kuchar вҖ” co-founder of BTC Prague, the largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe, and one of the most thoughtful voices on European Bitcoin policy вҖ” to break down why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe, why the European surveillance state is spreading faster than Americans realize, and why he believes Bitcoin must win because the alternative is just so much worse.

You'll learn:

вҶ’ How MiCA quietly killed 1,000+ Bitcoin-accepting restaurants in Czech Republic вҖ” and why it wasn't even a direct ban

вҶ’ Why Czech Republic became the Bitcoin capital of Europe (Trezor, Slush Pool, BIP-39, General Bytes вҖ” all started in Prague)

вҶ’ Why the petrodollar is dying вҖ” and what the loss of US hegemony means for global decentralization

вҶ’ Why Europeans use Bitcoin as money while Americans treat it as digital gold вҖ” and which approach is right

вҶ’ The European CBDC threat: pilots are launching THIS YEAR вҖ” and why most Americans haven't heard about it

вҶ’ The CBDC mechanism in Matyas's words: "You can analyze, you can control, you can surveil, you can send from a helicopter"

вҶ’ Why even an anarcho-capitalist now meets with politicians вҖ” and what changed his mind

вҶ’ Why Bitcoin is no longer just savings вҖ” it's the network of people building the future you actually want to live in

рҹҡЁ JOIN ME AT BTC PRAGUE 2026

The largest Bitcoin-only conference in Europe вҖ” June 11-13. Use code GREENCANDLE for discounted tickets:

https://btcprague.com/

рҹҺҜ PROTECT YOUR BITCOIN WITH SALT LENDING

Borrow against your Bitcoin without selling a single sat. Green Candle viewers get an exclusive offer:

https://saltlending.com/green-candle

рҹҺҜ PLAN YOUR WEALTH WITH STRONG WEALTH

Build a Bitcoin-aligned wealth strategy вҖ” estate planning, tax optimization, portfolio construction:

https://strongwealth.net/

рҹҺҜ MINE BITCOIN WITH SIMPLE MINING

First-year repairs free for Green Candle listeners:

https://simplemining.io/green-candle

рҹ“Ҡ REFERENCED IN THIS EPISODE

вҖў MiCA regulation (EU 2023/1114) вҖ” Markets in Crypto-Assets framework

вҖў Czech Republic Bitcoin tax exemption (3-year hold rule)

вҖў Slush Pool (founded 2010 in Prague, first ever Bitcoin mining pool)

вҖў BIP-39 seed phrase standard (developed in Prague)

вҖў Trezor hardware wallet (first ever, founded in Prague)

вҖў EU CBDC pilot programs (launching 2026)

вҖў BTC Prague 2026 conference вҖ” June 11-13

Bitcoin #BTCPrague #Matyas #MiCA #BitcoinEurope #CBDC #BitcoinPolicy #EuropeanBitcoin #BitcoinConference #SoundMoney #Petrodollar #BitcoinAdoption #CzechRepublic #FinancialFreedom #BitcoinNews

рҹ”” SUBSCRIBE for weekly Bitcoin, macro, and personal finance content.

вҡ пёҸ Disclaimer: This video is for informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Always do your own research.

item.description:
Im heutigen BTC-ECHO Invest analysieren Johannes Macswayed und Stefan LГјbeck, warum die jГјngsten Entwicklungen rund um den Iran-Deal, die StraГҹe von Hormus und die Г–lpreise fГјr Bitcoin relevant sind вҖ“ und weshalb die Datenlage weiterhin zur Vorsicht mahnt. AuГҹerdem sprechen wir Гјber den SpaceX-BГ¶rsengang, ETF-Flows, die Chancen auf eine Bodenbildung bei Bitcoin und den Гјberraschenden HГ¶henflug von Zcash.

рҹ“Ҳ Hat Bitcoin seinen Boden gefunden? рҹӣўпёҸ Warum fallende Г–lpreise die MГӨrkte bewegen рҹҡҖ Wie stark beeinflusst der SpaceX-Hype die LiquiditГӨt? рҹ’° Drehen die Bitcoin-ETFs wieder ins Positive? вҡЎ Zcash +1000 % in einem Jahr вҖ“ was steckt dahinter?

item.title: BitBucks Bitcoin Podcast вҖ“ Bitcoin und Deutschland: Wo stehen wir und wo kann die Reise hingehen? #6 mit Larry
item.description:
Herzlich willkommen zum BitBucks Bitcoin Podcast(https://www.bitbucks.de)!

Heute haben wir einen besonderen Gast: Larry, bekannt als @B1tcoin3r auf X. Larry ist ein erfahrener Bitcoin-Experte, Vortragsredner und Workshop-Host. Dank seines weitreichenden Netzwerks in fГјhrenden Positionen bringt er tiefgehende Einblicke in die aktuelle Bitcoin-Situation in Deutschland mit. Gemeinsam sprechen wir Гјber die deutsche Bitcoin-Politik, die Auswirkungen der neuen Regierung und die mГ¶gliche Zukunft von Bitcoin in Deutschland.

Viel SpaГҹ beim ZuhГ¶ren!

Zudem reden wir auch noch Гјber die Themen:

- Die aktuelle Bitcoin Lage in Deutschland (Adaption, Beliebtheit)

- LГ¶sungen fГјr eine Bitcoin Adaption

- Einfluss der Politik auf Bitcoin

- Ob Bitcoin die Politik Гјberhaupt braucht- Bitcoin und Unternehmen bzw. Start-Ups

- Wie viele Jahre es dauern wird, bis Deutschland Bitcoin als offizielles Zahlungsmittel einfГјhrt

- Bitcoin StГӨdte Spiel: Errate die fГјnf beliebtesten Bitcoin StГӨdte weltweit

- Wie Bitcoin Larrys Leben verГӨndert hat

- Glaskugel: Bitcoin Preis am Ende des Jahres 2025Podcast Folge auf Spotify:

---------------------------------------------------------------------

https://www.bitbucks.de/Apple Download: https://apps.apple.com/de/app/bitcoin-wallet-bitbucks/id1453167599

Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=de.fuf.bitbucks&pli=1

https://www.instagram.com/bit_bucks/

https://www.linkedin.com/company/bitbucks/

https://www.tiktok.com/@bitbucks

https://x.com/bit_bucksPodcast auf Spotify:

https://open.spotify.com/show/0bhDawE23ld6lPw7KnqUH5?si=5182b2525e7144db

----------------------------------------------------------------------

Vernetz dich gerne mit den Rednern :)

LinkedIn Konsti Schwemlein: https://www.linkedin.com/in/konstantin-schwemlein/

X/Twitter Larry: https://x.com/B1tcoin3r

--------------------------------------------------------------

TimeStamps:

00:00 Intro

01:15 Wie bist du zu Bitcoin gekommen?

03:20 Wie steht es um Bitcoin in Deutschland?

07:45 Thema Bitcoin: BevГ¶lkerung vs. Unternehmen/Politik

12:46 Was sind deine VerbesserungsvorschlГӨge?

15:15 Wie kann die deutsche Politik Bitcoin integrieren?

17:28 Gleicht eine Bitcoin Reserve die Staatsschulden aus?

19:50 Wird eine CDU Regierung das Thema Bitcoin fГ¶rdern?

22:38 Warum setzen sich die GrГјnen nicht fГјr Bitcoin ein?

27:18 Warum gibt es nicht mehr Bitcoin Unternehmen?

item.title: Bitcoin Audible вҖ“ The TRUTH about Iran's "Bitcoin" seizure
item.description:
I break down the TreasuryвҖҷs claim that it seized $1B of IranвҖҷs "crypto" and explain why this was actually Tether, not Bitcoin вҖ“ and why that distinction matters.

I digs into how stablecoins, ETFs, and exchanges recreate the same centralized, confiscable banking system, contrasted with true Bitcoin self-custody.

When you consider Iran, Russia, and Afghanistan as case studies, it is clear that every other reserve asset becomes politicized. The writing is on the wall for centralised finance, and the world will be forced onto Bitcoin because it is the only neutral money that simply keeps working.

item.title: MГјnzcrime - True Crime Bitcoin Podcast вҖ“ Unter GeldwГӨscheverdacht Episode 6 (mit Plep4Fun)
item.description:
Konto gesperrt. Komplett. Kein Гңberweisen, kein Abheben, kein gar nichts. Und das ohne Vorwarnung, ohne ErklГӨrung, einfach so. 


Genau das hat Pleb4Fun im Januar 2025 erwischt. Von einer Sekunde auf die andere war er raus aus dem System вҖ“ wГӨhrend Rechnungen weiterhin kamen und das Leben sich nicht um sein Bankproblem scherte. 
Was dann folgte, war kein schneller Anruf mit happy ending. Es war ein bГјrokratischer Alptraum, der eine viel unbequemere Frage aufwirft: Wie leicht gerГӨt man heutzutage eigentlich ins Visier der BehГ¶rden, obwohl man absolut nichts falsch gemacht hat? 


Chefermittler Mavi hat sich mit Pleb4Fun zusammengesetzt und die ganze Geschichte auseinandergenommen вҖ“ akribisch, ehrlich und ohne SchГ¶nfГӨrberei. Raus gekommen ist dabei eine Episode, die unter die Haut geht. Nicht weil sie reiГҹerisch ist, sondern weil es RealitГӨt ist.


GeldwГӨscheverdacht. Ein Begriff, den die meisten Menschen mit Kriminellen verbinden. Nicht mit jemandem, der einfach seinen Alltag lebt. 
Das hier ist keine abstrakte Diskussion Гјber Finanzgesetze. Das ist die Geschichte eines Menschen, der plГ¶tzlich beweisen musste, dass er kein Verbrecher ist вҖ“ und das Protokoll dazu liegt jetzt auf dem Tisch. 

Links zur Folge:
Pleb4Fun Dokumentation auf X

Ticketverkauf Event Brivat (Bitcoin Privacy Event)

Der Fall Samourai

V4V:
crime21@getalby.com

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Interviews вҖ“ Syed, Midnight President вҖ” $200M Spent, Zero VCs, and Why Anonymity Isn't Privacy
item.description: <![CDATA[Fahmi Syed runs the Midnight Foundation, custodian of a fourth-generation privacy-first blockchain backed by Charles Hoskinson. Roughly $200 million spent. Zero VC funding. One of the biggest airdrops in crypto history.Jamie Redman sits down with Fahmi at Consensus 2026 for the full breakdown: programmable privacy, selective disclosure, the dual-token Night/Dust model, why Zcash and Monero keep getting delisted, why agentic AI desperately needs a proof layer, and what "Web 2.5" actually means.We cover:- The bank-account-number anecdote that defines rational privacy- Why transparent blockchains can't carry institutions across the bridge- Midnight's private-permissions model between Monero and JPMorgan- The dual-token Night/Dust design and predictable corporate costs- One of crypto's biggest airdrops вҖ” no VCs, no insider allocations- March 2026 mainnet and the 100+ builders in pre-prod- The 2008 TradFi trading floor that shaped his self-custody viewsFilmed at Consensus 2026.Host: Jamie Redman]]>

item.title: Bald in Bitcoin вҖ“ Bald in Bitcoin - Folge 13: Die stille Steuer вҖ“ Warum der Staat Inflation nicht bekГӨmpfen will
item.description:
Deine Eltern haben dir gesagt: Leg Geld zurГјck. Sparen lohnt sich.

Guter Rat вҖ“ damals. Aber heute ist etwas kaputt.

In Folge 12 haben wir gezeigt, warum dein Kontostand stabil aussehen kann und du trotzdem ГӨrmer wirst. Heute gehen wir einen Schritt weiter und stellen die unbequeme Frage dahinter: Warum wird Inflation nicht einfach abgestellt?

Die Antwort: Weil das System genau so gebaut ist.

DAS LERNST DU IN DIESER FOLGE

вҖ“ Warum Staaten gigantische Schulden durch Inflation heimlich entwerten вҖ“ Was finanzielle Repression bedeutet вҖ“ und warum das kein VerschwГ¶rungsmythos ist вҖ“ Wer wirklich die Rechnung zahlt: die stille Steuer auf deine Ersparnisse вҖ“ Warum das 2%-Ziel der EZB keine zufГӨllige Zahl ist вҖ“ Was Bitcoin als mengenmГӨГҹig begrenztes System damit zu tun hat

WEITERFГңHRENDE EMPFEHLUNGEN

Der Bitcoin-Standard вҖ“ Saifedean Ammous Schulden: Die ersten 5000 Jahre вҖ“ David Graeber Reinhart & Sbrancia: The Liquidation of Government Debt (Studie, englisch)

VOR-ORT-KURS вҖ“ 30. MAI 2026

Bitcoin fГјr Einsteiger вҖ“ digitales Geld fГјr analog Geborene. Kein Trading. Kein Hype. Einfach ein ruhiger, verstГӨndlicher Einstieg. https://bitucation.com/kurs/vor-ort-bitcoin

Hinweis: In dieser Folge wird neben meiner Stimme eine KI-generierte Expertenstimme fГјr technische ErklГӨrpassagen eingesetzt. Alle Inhalte werden von mir recherchiert und geprГјft.

вҡ пёҸ Keine Finanzberatung. AusschlieГҹlich Bildung und persГ¶nliche Perspektive.

]]>

item.description:
In dieser Episode spricht Philipp mit Robin Seyr und Ben Raich im Auto auf dem Weg von San Salvador nach El Zonte Гјber ihre EindrГјcke aus El Salvador. Sie diskutieren die Bitcoin-Adoption, die Adopting Bitcoin-Konferenz, die Sicherheitslage, die alltГӨgliche Nutzung von Bitcoin und die politische Situation unter PrГӨsident Bukele.


Wer ist Robin Seyr?

Robin Seyr ist Host vom tГӨglichen Bitcoin Podcast.


Wer ist Ben Raich?

Ben Raich arbeitet mit Leidenschaft an vielfГӨltigen Projekten im Bitcoin-Space, um Bildung und Adaption von Bitcoin voranzutreiben. Sein Schwerpunkt liegt auf der Produktion hochwertiger Videopodcasts, dem Videoschnitt sowie journalistischen TГӨtigkeiten.

item.title: Epicenter - Learn about Crypto, Blockchain, Ethereum, Bitcoin and Distributed Technologies вҖ“ Why Only 3 Builders Control All of Ethereum
item.description:
In this episode, host Friederike Ernst is joined by Kubi Mensah, CEO and co-founder of Gattaca, the company behind Titan Builder. Kubi sheds light on the highly competitive and often opaque world of Ethereum block building, explaining how Gattaca evolved from a centralized exchange proprietary trading firm to one of the three dominant builders responsible for constructing the vast majority of Ethereum blocks today .
В 



В 
They dissect the true "journey of a transaction," revealing why over half of all Ethereum transaction value bypasses the public mempool in favor of private order flow auctions and MEV-protection services . Kubi explains the intricate mechanics of top-of-block bidding by high-frequency DeFi arbitrageurs, the necessity of extreme latency optimization, and the "flywheel effect" that makes block building a natural oligopoly . Finally, the discussion turns to the future of the Ethereum roadmap, unpacking how upcoming upgrades like ePBS (enshrined Proposer-Builder Separation) and FOCIL (Fork-Choice Enforced Inclusion Lists) aim to permanently alter the power dynamics between block builders, validators, and originators .
В 
В 
Topics


  • 04:15 Transitioning from TradFi Trading to Ethereum Block Building
  • 09:30 Redefining the Builder: Relays and Order Flow Auctions
  • 15:00 Unpacking the Mempool: Public vs. Private Transactions
  • 21:45 The Anatomy of a Block & "Top-of-Block" Arbitrage
  • 27:10 How Builders Pay Proposers to Win Auctions
  • 35:20 The Oligopoly: Why Only 3 Builders Dominate Ethereum
  • 42:15 Trust in the Dark Forest: Handling Searcher Bundles
  • 49:00 The Convergence of Builders and Solvers
  • 55:30 The Impact of ePBS, FOCIL, and Pre-confirmations




Sponsors:



Lido V3 introduces stVaults: a modular staking infrastructure that lets builders and institutions deploy custom staking vaults, while staying anchored to stETH as a shared liquidity layer.Get started building with Lido V3 today: https://lido.fi/stvaults?mtm_campaign=epicenterBlock Space Forum: https://blockspace.forum/

]]>

item.title: Bitcoin.com News Weekly Update вҖ“ Bitcoin, Oil, Saylor and SpaceX: The Weekly Market Shock
item.description: <![CDATA[This week on The Weekly, David Sencil and Graham Stone break down a major set of stories moving Bitcoin and crypto markets.They discuss the reported Iran-US peace deal, the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, falling oil futures, and BitcoinвҖҷs move above $66K. They also unpack Michael SaylorвҖҷs controversial 32 BTC sale, his BTC Prague comments, MetaplanetвҖҷs move into Bitcoin yield products, Bitcoin miners pivoting toward AI, AnthropicвҖҷs Fable export-control controversy, and the SpaceX IPOвҖҷs impact on crypto-based pre-IPO price discovery.A fast-moving episode covering macro, Bitcoin, AI, miners, IPOs, and the next stage of crypto market structure.]]>

item.title: Bitcoin Rails | Isabel Foxen Duke вҖ“ BIP 110 & The Future of Soft Forks | SUPER TESTNET
item.description:
One of Bitcoin's most prolific experimental developers, Super Testnet is known for building software at the frontier of Bitcoin research вҖ” including notable contributions to BitVM, Drivechains, Lightning, and numerous prototypes that have pushed the boundaries of Bitcoin's design space.

More recently, he's become a prominent participant in discussions surrounding BIP 110, a proposed soft fork intended to restrict transaction patterns associated with Ordinal inscriptions.

While Super broadly agrees with the proposal's underlying goals, he argues its current restrictions are overly broad and could impact "legitimate" monetary uses of Bitcoin.

These concerns led him to share a User-Rejected Soft Fork proposal last week, creating a rare case study in how Bitcoin's consensus rules evolve in practice.

In more detail, Super Testnet & I discuss:

  • BIP 110, and why developers disagree over transaction filtering
  • Soft fork activation through a historical lens
  • Miner-Activated and User-Activated Soft Forks + the hybrid strategy proposed by BIP 110 advocates
  • Super's User-Rejected Soft Fork proposal and its game theoretical implications for BIP 110 activation
  • Novel signaling mechanisms for BIP 110 + their implications for the future of hashrate markets

This episode of Bitcoin Rails is brought to you by my sponsors:

LayerTwo Labs вҖ” developing research, software, and technologies for scaling Bitcoin via the integration of Drivechains (BIP 300/301)

Hashi on Sui Network вҖ” a primitive for executing Bitcoin Defi transactions, without having to trust a federated bridge or other centralized entity

BitBox вҖ” an open-source Bitcoin-only hardware wallet, with smooth UX and no compromises on security. Check out Bitbox [dot] swiss and use code BITCOINRAILS to get a discount

]]>

item.title: Surfin' Bitcoin вҖ“ Qui contrГҙlera votre argent dans 10 ans ?
item.description:

Guerre commerciale ou guerre monГ©taire ?

PrivilГЁge exorbitant du dollar, excГ©dents allemands et japonais, dГ©mographie et bloc des В« vieux В» contre bloc des В« jeunes В», stablecoins et CLARITY Act, euro numГ©rique, dollarisation des pays Г©mergents, Bitcoin hors systГЁmeвҖҰ

item.title: BitcoinBГЎzis - Minden, Ami KriptГі вҖ“ Hogyan KГ©szГӯtsГјk Fel a Fiatalokat az AI Korszakra? | Brezovszki MГЎtГ©
item.description:
A BitcoinBГЎzis legГәjabb epizГіdjГЎban LЕ‘rincz Barbara Brezovszki MГЎtГ©val beszГ©lget, aki korГЎbban banki karriert hagyott ott a blockchain vilГЎgГЎГ©rt, majd DeFi-projekteket, neobankot Г©s szabГЎlyozГЎsi tapasztalatot szerzett. Most pedig az AI Г©s a fiatal generГЎciГі jГ¶vЕ‘jГ©t Г©pГӯti.


рҹ“ҢMirЕ‘l szГіl az adГЎs?


MГЎtГ© elmesГ©li вҖңOrange pillвҖқ tГ¶rtГ©netГ©t, elsЕ‘ blockchain projektjeit, a szabГЎlyozГЎs kihГӯvГЎsait Г©s azt, miГ©rt lГЎt hatalmas lehetЕ‘sГ©get az AI-ban a fiataloknГЎl.


Bemutatja legГәjabb kezdemГ©nyezГ©sГ©t, a Project Vibe-ot: egy nyГЎri programot 13-17 Г©ves tiniknek, ahol AI-eszkГ¶zГ¶kkel valГіdi termГ©keket Г©pГӯtenek, startup-mГіdszertant tanulnak Г©s kritikus gondolkodГЎst gyakorolnak.


KГјlГ¶n kiemelik, miГ©rt fontos mГЎr tinikorban elkezdeni a вҖңbuilderвҖқ gondolkodГЎsmГіdot, hogyan lehet legyЕ‘zni az AI-t (Beat the AI challenge), Г©s milyen akadГЎlyok nehezГӯtik ma MagyarorszГЎgon a fiatal vГЎllalkozГіkat.


IdЕ‘bГ©lyegek:

00:00 - 00:30 Intro

00:31 - 02:12 MГЎtГ© вҖңOrange pillвҖқ pillanata

02:13 - 02:51 Bitpanda

27:48 - 28:35 FireFish

33:41 - 38:11 Kripto Г©s MiCA szabГЎlyozГЎs 2025-2026-ban

38:12 - 41:12 Plusz tГЎbor infГіk

41:13 - 42:00 ElГ©rhetЕ‘sГ©gek, outro


рҹ“Ң KapcsolГіdГі linkek:

рҹ”— Project Vibe: www.projectvibe.hu

Beat the AI Challenge вҖ“ Nyerd meg az ingyenes vagy kedvezmГ©nyes rГ©szvГ©telt!

вҡЎHa Г©rdekel a jГ¶vЕ‘, az AI, a startupok Г©s hogy hogyan kГ©szГӯtsГјk fel a gyerekeinket a kГ¶vetkezЕ‘ Г©vtizedre, ez az epizГіd neked szГіl!


рҹ‘Қ Nyomj egy lГЎjkot, iratkozz fel Г©s kapcsold be a csengЕ‘t, hogy ne maradj le a kГ¶vetkezЕ‘ rГ©szekrЕ‘l!

item.title: Der Relai Bitcoin Podcast вҖ“ Bitcoins Einfluss auf globale KapitalmГӨrkte, mit Leon Wankum | Relai Bitcoin Podcast #123
item.description:
Leon Wankum ist der Head of Bitcoin Strategies bei oneVest, einem Familienunternehmen mit Fokus auf Immobilienentwicklung und Investments. Leon ist jedoch auch aktiv in der Bitcoin-Community und ist regelmГӨssig in Podcasts zu hГ¶ren oder schreibt seinen eigenen Newsletter. Nun hat er auch sein eigenes Bitcoin-Buch verГ¶ffentlicht.

Im GesprГӨch mit Relai-CEO Julian Liniger erzГӨhlt Leon von seiner Motivation, ein Bitcoin-Buch zu schreiben, weshalb ihn Bitcoin nach Гјber 10 Jahren immer noch so motiviert, wie er die Lage rund um Bitcoin auf der Bilanz sieht, und wohin Bitcoin in den kommenden Jahren noch gehen wird.

Folge Leon auf X: https://x.com/leonwankum

Bestelle sein Buch hier: https://store.bitcoinmagazine.com/pages/digital-real-estate-pre-order

Oder besuche seine Webseite: https://leonwankum.com/

вҖ”

вҸұ Kapitel:

00:00 - Intro und Vorstellung

00:50 - Bitcoin und digitales Immobilieninvestment

03:28 - Bitcoin als Grundlage fГјr Wohlstand

09:25 - Metaplanet und Bitcoin als Kapital

12:14 - Werbeunterbrechung fГјr die Relai-App

16:45 - StrategyвҖҷs neue Finanzinstrumente

23:50 - Bitcoin im globalen Finanzsystem

34:10 - Abschluss und Ausblick

вҖ”

Willst du dir die Sendung lieber anhГ¶ren?

https://podcasts.apple.com/at/podcast/der-relai-bitcoin-podcast/id1543240691

рҹҺ§Auf Spotify anhГ¶ren:

https://open.spotify.com/show/4nwQa2HLv2d9GHnGm6GpoO?si=2bd070927706437d

вҖ”

Die in der Sendung geГӨusserten Meinungen sind die Meinungen der einzelnen GГӨste und geben nicht unbedingt die Meinung der Relai AG wieder. Die Informationen im Podcast kГ¶nnen ohne VorankГјndigung geГӨndert werden, und es wird nicht garantiert, dass sie richtig, vollstГӨndig oder aktuell sind.

вҖ”

рҹҺ“ Lerne Bitcoin besser kennen:

Nach Abschluss der Passporting-Verfahren baut das Unternehmen seine Dienstleistungen aktiv auf weitere EU-Mitgliedstaaten aus.

item.title: Thank God for Bitcoin вҖ“ To The Unknown Pod Episode 42: Bitcoin and A World At War
item.description:
In this episode, Jordan Bush and Ryan Finlay explore the complex intersections of global conflict, economic warfare, and Bitcoin's role as a resilient alternative in a world at war. They analyze recent Middle East tensions, the decline of the petrodollar, and how Bitcoin offers a form of financial sovereignty amidst geopolitical chaos.

]]>
